executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware crisis with over 100 Canadian organizations publicly listed as victims in the past 48 hours, including critical infrastructure like Peterborough Public Health, Eastern Townships School Board, and Municipality of North Perth. The Qilin ransomware group alone has claimed 20+ Canadian victims, followed by Play, Clop, and SafePay operations. High-profile targets include Canada Goose, National Money Mart (database leaked), Morison Insurance Brokers, and telecommunications provider Eeyou Communications Network. Additionally, CCCS has issued AL26-011 alerting to two new Linux vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-43284 and CVE-2026-43500) affecting Canadian systems. This represents a coordinated, multi-group assault on Canadian critical infrastructure and businesses across all sectors.

items.description: Vulnerability affecting Linux systems (details pending CCCS advisory review)
Vulnerability affecting Linux systems (details pending CCCS advisory review)

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is experiencing a severe ransomware epidemic with over 100 organizations publicly compromised across all sectors and provinces in the past 48 hours. CCCS issued Alert AL26-011 on May 8th regarding two Linux vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-43284, CVE-2026-43500) requiring immediate attention. Critical infrastructure victims include public health agencies, municipalities, school boards, telecommunications providers, and a Quebec regional district government.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (consumer services, claimed by Coinbasecartel ransomware), Active Green + Ross (automotive retail, claimed by Sinobi)
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company (database leaked by Everest ransomware), Morison Insurance Brokers (claimed by Sinobi), Trucash (claimed by Lynx), multiple accounting firms including Pewarchuk CPA, MG CPA

report_2w.executive_summary: Palo Alto Networks firewalls are under active attack via CVE-2026-0300, a critical remote code execution flaw that has been exploited in the wild since April 9, giving attackers root-level access for espionage. Linux systems face a critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-31431, dubbed 'Copy Fail') now patched but widely deployed. The Canadian government issued three advisories in the past week, including warnings about social engineering attacks on enterprise SaaS platforms. Iranian state-sponsored group MuddyWater is using Microsoft Teams and fake ransomware to disguise espionage operations, while ShinyHunters leaked data from multiple organizations including Cushman & Wakefield and Woflow (448K accounts exposed).

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued three advisories in the past week: AL26-009 for the critical Linux 'Copy Fail' vulnerability (CVE-2026-31431), AL26-010 warning of social engineering attacks compromising enterprise SaaS environments, and AL26-008 for cPanel/WHM vulnerabilities. The Canadian government also agreed to pay $8.7M to settle a class-action lawsuit related to a CRA account data breach. Privacy commissioners found that ChatGPT collected 'vast amounts' of Canadians' data without consent.
